skill.name: react-native-best-practices
skill.description: Guides React Native performance optimization for FPS, startup time, bundle size, memory, animations, lists, native modules, and Android release readiness.
skill.summary: Improve React Native app performance with targeted profiling and optimization guidance.
skill.author: callstackincubator

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only React Native performance skill. Static analysis mostly matched Markdown fences, shell examples, and performance terminology, not executable skill code. Publication is acceptable with warnings for pipe-to-shell installation guidance and remote chunk loading examples.

medium_findings.title: Pipe-To-Shell Installer Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends installing Flashlight with a curl-to-bash command. This is not malicious, but it executes downloaded code without inspection and should be shown as a warning.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell command appears twice in a performance measurement guide. The context is legitimate tooling, but the installation pattern is clearly risky.

medium_findings.title: Remote Chunk Loading Requires Trust Controls
medium_findings.description: The code splitting guide shows production chunks loaded from a CDN and module federation examples. This is legitimate Re.Pack usage, but users need integrity and release controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The guidance intentionally loads runtime code from configured URLs. No malicious endpoint is present, but remote code loading is security-sensitive by design.

low_findings.title: Markdown Shell Examples Flagged As Execution
low_findings.description: Most external command alerts are Markdown code fences that document normal React Native, Expo, Android, and iOS tooling. They are not executable skill logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are documentation blocks and lookup commands. They do not run automatically when the skill is loaded.

low_findings.title: Keylogger Keyword False Positive
low_findings.description: The critical keylogger alerts come from the word keystroke in a UI latency example. The content discusses blocked typing, not logging user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding examples use TextInput to explain responsiveness with useDeferredValue. No capture, storage, or transmission of keystrokes is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Alerts Are Performance Guidance
low_findings.description: The crypto-related alerts are documentation about replacing slow JavaScript crypto packages with native implementations. No weak algorithm or credential handling was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content compares package choices for performance and recommends CSPRNG-capable native crypto. It does not implement encryption or request secrets.

low_findings.title: Environment And Filesystem Access Are Configuration Examples
low_findings.description: Environment and filesystem alerts are examples for build flags, cold-start detection, cache paths, mmap explanation, and local dependency inspection. No secret exfiltration was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples read feature flags or explain platform behavior. The skill does not combine these with network transmission or credential collection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe-To-Shell Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The command pattern downloads a script and immediately executes it. Users should verify the installer source or use a package manager when possible.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: This is a direct curl-to-bash pattern in documentation. It is not evidence of malicious intent, but it is a well-known unsafe execution pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Runtime Code Loading From Remote Locations
dangerous_patterns.description: The Re.Pack examples configure app chunks from remote URLs. This requires trusted hosting, version pinning, and rollback controls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples intentionally show remote module and chunk loading. The placeholder domains are not malicious, but the pattern affects application trust boundaries.

content.value_statement: React Native teams often lose time finding the real cause of slow screens, jank, large bundles, and leaks. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code targeted guidance for profiling and fixing performance issues.
actual_capabilities: Maps common React Native performance problems to focused reference files.
Explains FPS measurement, React profiling, startup timing, and memory investigation workflows.
Provides bundle size guidance for source-map-explorer, Expo Atlas, R8, tree shaking, and asset handling.
Covers React patterns including FlashList, atomic state, concurrent rendering, uncontrolled inputs, and Reanimated worklets.
Documents native performance topics such as Turbo Modules, threading, view flattening, memory patterns, and Android alignment.
limitations: It is documentation, not an automated profiler or benchmark runner.
Some workflows require manual review of screenshots, treemaps, or native profiler output.
Version guidance may need verification against the user project and current React Native releases.
Several examples include shell commands that users should review before running.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Janky Screens
use_cases.description: Find whether dropped frames come from JavaScript work, native rendering, list rendering, or animation setup.

use_cases.title: Reduce Release Size
use_cases.description: Analyze JavaScript and native bundle size, then choose practical fixes for imports, assets, R8, and tree shaking.

use_cases.title: Review Native Performance Choices
use_cases.description: Evaluate Turbo Modules, native SDK choices, threading behavior, and Android platform requirements before shipping.

prompt_templates.title: Find The Right Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: My React Native app feels slow during scrolling and input. Use this skill to choose the best references and explain what to check first.

prompt_templates.title: Profile A Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this screen for React Native performance issues. Focus on re-renders, list rendering, expensive computation, and animation work.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Bundle Reduction
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a bundle size reduction plan for my React Native app. Include measurement steps, likely offenders, and safe optimization order.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Native Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my native module and startup architecture. Use this skill to assess TTI, threading, Hermes mmap, memory ownership, and Android alignment risks.

output_examples.input: The search screen stutters while users type into a filter box.
output_examples.output: The response identifies JavaScript thread pressure, recommends React profiling, suggests deferring expensive list updates, and checks TextInput control patterns.

output_examples.input: The Android release grew after adding several dependencies.
output: Build a production bundle and inspect the treemap.
Check for barrel imports, duplicate packages, and large polyfills.
Review native assets and R8 configuration.

output_examples.input: A team wants to add remote chunks with Re.Pack.
output_examples.output: The response explains when code splitting helps, warns about Hermes tradeoffs, and calls out CDN trust, caching, and version control requirements.

best_practices: Measure first with production-like builds before changing architecture or libraries.
Optimize the highest-impact path first, usually FPS, startup, bundle size, or memory.
Treat shell commands and remote loading examples as guidance that needs project review.
anti_patterns: Do not apply every optimization without confirming the actual bottleneck.
Do not migrate to Re.Pack or remote chunks only for minor bundle concerns.
Do not run installer commands from documentation without verifying the source.

faq.question: Does this skill run profiling tools automatically?
faq.answer: No. It explains how to use profiling tools and how to interpret common results.

faq.question: Can it help with Expo and bare React Native apps?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for Expo, React Native CLI, Hermes, Re.Pack, iOS, and Android.

faq.question: Is the skill only about JavaScript performance?
faq.answer: No. It also covers native modules, threading, startup time, native memory, assets, and Android release checks.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: Most alerts are false positives, but the documentation includes pipe-to-shell and remote code loading examples.

faq.question: Does it replace human performance testing?
faq.answer: No. Users still need real devices, release builds, profiler output, and product-specific acceptance criteria.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
